stories.title: D11 Orton Academy Moving Into Trailblazer Elementary — Some Parents Blindsided
stories.angle: Colorado Springs District 11 has announced that Orton Academy — a K-8 charter school specializing in dyslexia education — will co-locate at Trailblazer Elementary starting fall 2026. Trailblazer was built for 600 students and has an enrollment of about 220, leaving room for the move. Orton Academy will be D11's ninth charter school. Some Trailblazer parents felt blindsided after hearing about the move from Orton's website before receiving official district communication. The district held an information session April 9 at 6 PM at the school.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D11 parents: Orton Academy (dyslexia-focused K-8 charter) is co-locating at Trailblazer Elementary this fall. Both schools run independently. Trailblazer capacity: ~600, current enrollment: ~220.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Some Trailblazer parents found out about the Orton Academy co-location from Orton's website — not from D11. The district held an info session April 9. If you have questions, contact D11 directly.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D11 families with kids at Trailblazer Elementary — here's the news making the rounds this week.

Starting fall 2026, Orton Academy will co-locate at Trailblazer Elementary. Orton Academy is a K-8 charter school chartered by D11 that focuses exclusively on educating students with dyslexia. It opened in 2020 and became D11's ninth charter school when it officially entered the district's charter contract in 2024.

The move makes sense on a facilities level: Trailblazer was built for about 600 students but currently serves around 220. The two schools will operate separately — Orton will have its own staff, students, and classrooms within the building.

What frustrated some Trailblazer parents was how they found out. Several families said they learned about the announcement through Orton's website before receiving any official communication from D11. The district held an information session on April 9 at 6 PM at the school.

If you have questions, reach out to D11 directly or check the district website. We'll keep you updated as more details come out.
facebook.image_caption: D11 news: Orton Academy (K-8 dyslexia-focused charter school, ~220 students) is moving into Trailblazer Elementary for fall 2026. Trailblazer capacity is ~600 — plenty of room. Both schools operate separately with their own classrooms and staff.

stories.title: CPSC Warning — HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil, Poisoning Risk to Young Children
stories.angle: The CPSC has issued a warning (not a formal recall) urging parents to immediately stop using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil. The oil contains methyl salicylate, which requires child-resistant packaging under federal law — but the product's packaging is NOT child-resistant, posing a serious poisoning risk if a young child accesses it. The product was sold on Amazon from May 2022 through August 2025 for about $10. Parents who have this product should secure it out of reach of children.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
CPSC warning: Stop using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil. Packaging is not child-resistant — wintergreen contains methyl salicylate, toxic to kids in small amounts. ~$10 on Amazon. Poison Control: 1-800-222-1222.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Parents, do a quick check of your essential oil cabinet today.

The CPSC has issued a warning to immediately stop using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil. The reason: the packaging is NOT child-resistant, and wintergreen oil contains methyl salicylate — a compound that is toxic to young children even in small amounts.

The product was sold on Amazon from May 2022 through August 2025 for about $10. It comes in white and blue packaging with an amber bottle, screw cap, and dropper. "HiQiLi High Quality Life" and "Wintergreen Essential Oil" are printed on the labels.

The CPSC's guidance is simple: stop using it, and secure it completely out of reach of children.

If you bought this oil in the past few years and still have it in your home, take action now. And if a child ever gets into any essential oil — call Poison Control immediately at 1-800-222-1222 (free, 24/7).

Do you keep essential oils at home? Have you ever thought about how they're stored around little ones?
facebook.image_caption: Safety alert: CPSC says stop using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil immediately. Non-child-resistant packaging. Sold on Amazon ~$10. Contains methyl salicylate — toxic to young kids. Secure out of reach of children. Questions? 1-800-222-1222.

article.headline: CPSC Says Stop Using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil — Here's Why It's a Risk to Young Children

image_concepts.label: Image 2A
image_concepts.usage: X Post 2A, FB Image Caption 10 AM

research.facts:
CPSC Warning — HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil Poisoning Risk
**Sources:**
- cpsc.gov — "CPSC Warns Consumers to Immediately Stop Using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Poisoning to Young Children; Violates Mandatory Standard for Child-Resistant Packaging"
- facebook.com/USCPSC — CPSC official Facebook post confirming warning
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- CPSC issued a Warning (not a formal recall) to stop using HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil (cpsc.gov)
- Product contains methyl salicylate, which requires child-resistant packaging under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (cpsc.gov)
- The product's packaging is NOT child-resistant, violating mandatory standards (cpsc.gov)
- Sold online on Amazon.com from May 2022 through August 2025 for about $10 (cpsc.gov)
- Product identification: "HiQiLi High Quality Life" and "Wintergreen Essential Oil" printed on front labels of packaging and both bottles (cpsc.gov)
- Comes in white and blue packaging; amber bottle with screw cap and dropper cap; travel-size bottle also included (cpsc.gov)
- CPSC recommends: immediately stop using, secure out of reach of children (cpsc.gov)
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- No injuries noted in search results; this is a preventive warning (likely accurate but not confirmed from 2 independent sources)
**Freshness:** Recent CPSC warning. Product sold through August 2025 means many households may still have this. Timely safety story.

stories.title: Starsmore Visitor Center Spring Construction — What COS Families Need to Know
stories.angle: The City of Colorado Springs began improvements at the Starsmore Visitor and Nature Center (North Cheyenne Cañon Park) on March 9, with construction tentatively scheduled through May 22, 2026. Improvements include a new outdoor plaza and classroom, ADA-compliant restrooms, native plant garden, and upgraded parking. During construction: S. Cheyenne Canyon Road section adjacent to the facility is closed; Parking Lot 1 ("Pull-Off 1") is closed. Parking Lot 2 ("Pull-Off 2") via Evans Ave remains open. The visitor center stays open except during limited hazardous construction activities.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Heading to North Cheyenne Cañon this spring? Starsmore is under construction through ~May 22. Lot 1 closed — use Lot 2 via Evans Ave. Center stays open. New plaza, ADA restrooms, and native garden coming.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Heads up before your next trip to North Cheyenne Cañon Park.

The Starsmore Visitor and Nature Center is in the middle of a spring construction project that started March 9 and is scheduled to wrap up around May 22. The work is exciting — they're adding a new outdoor plaza and classroom space, ADA-compliant restrooms, a native plant garden with interpretive signs, and better parking for school buses. But while construction is happening, things look a little different on the ground.

Here's what you need to know right now:
- Parking Lot 1 ("Pull-Off 1") is closed
- A section of S. Cheyenne Canyon Road near the facility is closed most of the time during construction
- Parking Lot 2 ("Pull-Off 2") via Evans Ave is open and accessible
- The Visitor and Nature Center itself stays open (except during limited hazardous construction activities)

So if you're planning a family hike up the Cañon this spring, the park is still very much open — just go in ready for the alternate parking setup. The upgrades are going to be worth the short-term hassle. Have you been to Starsmore recently? What's your family's favorite trail up there?
facebook.image_caption: North Cheyenne Cañon update: Starsmore Visitor Center is under construction through ~May 22. Parking Lot 1 closed. Use Lot 2 via Evans Ave. Center stays open. New plaza, ADA restrooms, and native plant garden coming soon.

article.headline: What COS Families Need to Know Before Visiting North Cheyenne Cañon This Spring

stories.title: COS Trail Improvements Spring 2026 — Pikes Peak Greenway and Rock Island Trail
stories.angle: Two significant trail improvement projects are underway in Colorado Springs this spring. On the Pikes Peak Greenway corridor, deteriorating asphalt is being replaced with about 0.5 miles of new concrete trail (in partnership with Stormwater Engineering, Monument Creek area). On the Rock Island Trail, a 210-foot pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek is under construction — a 12-month project starting spring 2026 — that will connect the west and east sides of the trail and add a pedestrian-activated flashing crossing at Peterson Road.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Two COS trail upgrades this spring:
Pikes Peak Greenway — ~0.5 mi new concrete near Monument Creek
Rock Island Trail — 210-ft bridge over Sand Creek (12-month project)

Better access for strollers, bikes, everyone.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Great news for Colorado Springs families who love to get outside: two significant trail improvement projects are happening this spring that are going to make a real difference for strollers, bikes, and walkers.

First, on the Pikes Peak Greenway corridor near Monument Creek, the city is replacing deteriorating asphalt with about half a mile of new concrete trail. This is a joint project with Stormwater Engineering. If you've pushed a stroller or ridden a bike on that stretch, you know why this matters.

Second, and this one's a big deal — a 210-foot pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek is under construction on the Rock Island Trail. This bridge will connect the west and east sides of the trail, which has been a gap in the network for a while. The project also includes a pedestrian-activated flashing crossing at Peterson Road. It's a 12-month project, so the bridge should be done sometime in spring 2027 — but the work is underway now.

Both projects are part of the city's 2026 parks, trails, and open space investment plan. Colorado Springs has more than 200 miles of trails, and improvements like these make them more accessible and enjoyable for everyone — families included.

Which COS trail is your family's favorite?
facebook.image_caption: Two big trail upgrades in Colorado Springs this spring: a new concrete section on the Pikes Peak Greenway (Monument Creek area) and a 210-ft pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek on the Rock Island Trail. Better trails for families, strollers, and bikes.

article.headline: Two Major COS Trail Upgrades Are Happening This Spring — Here's What Families Can Expect

- krdo.com — "Colorado Springs unveils 2026 plan to expand trails and improve open spaces" (January 22, 2026)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**

stories.title: Wintergreen Essential Oil Safety Pattern — What Every Parent Needs to Know
stories.angle: Essential oils are everywhere in Colorado Springs households. Wintergreen oil in particular poses a specific danger to young children because it contains high concentrations of methyl salicylate — a compound toxic to children even in small amounts. The CPSC has acted against multiple wintergreen oil brands for non-child-resistant packaging (HiQiLi warning 2026, GM Gumili recall 2026). This article gives parents a practical checklist: what to look for, how to store essential oils safely, and what to do if a child ingests them.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
CPSC has flagged 3 wintergreen oil brands in 2025-2026 for non-child-resistant packaging. Wintergreen contains methyl salicylate — toxic to kids in tiny amounts. Lock it away. Poison Control: 1-800-222-1222.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you use essential oils at home — and plenty of us do — this is worth a few minutes of your time.

The CPSC has taken action against three different wintergreen essential oil brands since 2025 (HiQiLi, GM Gumili, and Euqee) — all for the same reason: non-child-resistant packaging. Wintergreen oil looks and smells harmless. But it contains methyl salicylate, a compound that the CPSC specifically regulates because it's toxic to children even in small amounts.

We're not saying your diffuser is a hazard. We're saying that the bottle matters, and where you store it matters.

Here's a practical checklist for any family that keeps essential oils in the house:

• Store essential oils in a locked cabinet or on a shelf completely out of a child's reach
• Always replace caps immediately after using
• Never leave open bottles unattended when kids are around
• If you order essential oils online (especially from Amazon), check whether the product came in child-resistant packaging
• If a child ingests any essential oil — call Poison Control immediately: 1-800-222-1222 (free, 24/7, available in Spanish and English)

The more you know, the safer your home is. That's true for all of us.
facebook.image_caption: The CPSC has flagged 3 wintergreen essential oil brands in 2025-2026 for non-child-resistant packaging. Wintergreen oil can be toxic to kids. Store in a locked cabinet. If ingested: call Poison Control 1-800-222-1222.

article.headline: Wintergreen Essential Oil Safety: What Every Parent Needs to Know After a Wave of CPSC Actions

image_concepts.label: Image 5A
image_concepts.usage: X Post 5A, FB Image Caption 5 PM

research.facts:
Wintergreen Essential Oil Safety — What Parents Need to Know
**Sources:**
- cpsc.gov — HiQiLi Wintergreen Essential Oil Warning (2026)
- cpsc.gov — GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil Bottles Recalled (2026, sold on Amazon by Gumili Beauty)
- cpsc.gov — Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oils Recalled (2025, Amazon by DLWL-US)
- abc27.com — "Thousands of essential oils recalled due to risk of poisoning"
- cpsc.gov — Poison Prevention Information Center (general methyl salicylate resource)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Wintergreen essential oil contains methyl salicylate, which is toxic to children (cpsc.gov — Poison Prevention + multiple recall pages; 2+ sources)
- Methyl salicylate must be in child-resistant packaging under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (cpsc.gov — multiple recall pages)
- CPSC has taken action against multiple wintergreen oil brands in 2025-2026: HiQiLi (Warning, 2026), GM Gumili (Recall, 2026), Euqee (Recall, 2025) — 3 separate CPSC actions (cpsc.gov — 3 sources)
- GM Gumili: bottles sold on Amazon by Gumili Beauty; violated mandatory child-resistant packaging standard; recall 2026 (cpsc.gov)
- CPSC Poison Prevention page provides general guidance on essential oil storage and child safety (cpsc.gov)
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Methyl salicylate is rapidly absorbed through skin and mucous membranes; even small amounts can be toxic to children (general toxicology knowledge, consistent with CPSC warning language)
**Safety guidance (for article practical section):**
- Store all essential oils in locked cabinet or high shelf, not reachable by children
- Never leave caps off essential oil bottles
- If child ingests: call Poison Control immediately 1-800-222-1222
- Do not induce vomiting — call Poison Control first
**Freshness:** Pattern of 2025-2026 CPSC actions against wintergreen oil brands; many COS families use essential oils; actionable safety guide content. Not covered in story-history.md.
